content: For a single-worker FastAPI app with no Redis, the standard lightweight pattern to avoid repeated expensive async calls (e.g. LLM completions) on every poll is a manual in-process dict cache keyed by the inputs that determine the result, checked before the expensive call and populated after — no cache library needed for a single-process demo app; libraries like async_lru or fastapi-cache2 exist for larger apps but add dependency weight not justified here.
